title: Reconstruct absolute tyre compounds and race-level strategy priors
summary: Current features treat `SOFT/MEDIUM/HARD` as globally comparable, but Pirelli only labels the weekend-relative trio; `SOFT` can mean C3 at Qatar or C5 at Las Vegas/Abu Dhabi.
rationale: Current features treat `SOFT/MEDIUM/HARD` as globally comparable, but Pirelli only labels the weekend-relative trio; `SOFT` can mean C3 at Qatar or C5 at Las Vegas/Abu Dhabi. Official guides also frame races as one-stop vs two-stop biased based on track characteristics and compound nomination, so the same observed tyre life can imply very different stop risk by race.
implementation_hint: Build a `Race_Year -> {hard_C, medium_C, soft_C}` lookup from public Pirelli/F1 nomination pages, then map each row’s `Compound` to an absolute softness index. Add features like absolute compound rank, weekend softness span, `TyreLife / expected_life_by_absolute_compound`, race one-stop/two-stop prior, and interactions with `RaceProgress` and `LapsRemaining_Est`. Validate with grouped folds by race-year so lookup priors do not leak.
expected_effect: Should improve generalization on cross-race shifts where current heuristics overfit relative colour labels instead of true compound hardness.
risk: Requires a clean race-name/year join and manual coverage for all weekends in train/test; gains may be modest if current `Race` features already proxy most of this.
